rulings.Case Title: SAMIR HINN ET AL VS. DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Settlement And Application For Determination Of Good Fatih Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF SAMIR HINN, RAMIZ HINN, JOSEPH HINN Plaintiffs Notice Of Settlement And Application For Determination Of Good Fatih Settlement is CONTINUED to July 10, 2023, to be heard after the issuance of a ruling on Petition to Approve Settlement in a related case, PTR-11-294215, which is currently scheduled to be heard on July 3, 2023.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LUMINOUS ION LLC VS. VICKI CHIAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of Order Compelling Arbitration Of Claims Against Vicki Chiao, Et Al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF LUMINOUS ION LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of Order Compelling Arbitration Of Claims Against Vicki Chiao, Et Al is DENIED. While Plaintiff styled their motion as a Motion for Clarification, this is really a Motion to Compel Discovery from Third Parties. As the Special Master appointed to this case pointed out, "Chiao, 367 Liberty, and MVC are not participants in the subject litigation, by neither having answered or filing a Notice of appearance." However, that motion is not before the Court.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IMC PRIVATE CAPITAL, LLC VS. DON RAMON'S REAL ESTATE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation And Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF IMC PRIVATE CAPITAL, LLC Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation And Settlement Hearing Required. The settlement agreement is ambiguous as to whether the landlord may increase rent. The Court must take evidence as to Defendant Lionela Ramirez's intention regarding the amount of rent when the settlement agreement was signed. Parties to be present in court or via zoom.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT AMBERLY PENNOCK HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION HEARING REQUIRED. rulings.Case Title: SHAWNY K. WILLIAMS VS. SHANDA LOVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Shannon Williams; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2023 line 8. PLAINTIFF SHAWNY WILLIAMS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Shannon Williams is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Parties are ordered to meet and confer at 8:30am in person. Complete, verified responses to Form Interrogatories - General (Set One), Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), and Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) shall be provided by Defendant Shannon Williams within 5 days of notice of entry of this order without objections. All matters in Requests for Admission (Set One) are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $462.50. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
